title: Union with multiple binds produces unexpected results
description:
Virtuoso Version: 07.10.3209
select distinct ?direct ?link where {

    ?0 a <tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#Office> .

    {

        ?0 ?link [] bind (true as ?direct)

    } union {

        [] ?link ?0 bind (false as ?direct)

    }

}

Expected Results
direct  link
true    http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type
true    http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#area
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#city
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#code
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#country
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#employee
true    tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#state
false   tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#office

Actual Results
direct  link
        http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#type
        http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#area
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#city
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#code
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#country
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#employee
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#state
        tag:metreeca.net;2013:birt#office


Fine without union.
Likely related to #212 and #252.
key_bug_features: Union with multiple binds in query produces unexpected results
bug_feature_seed: SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> INSERT DATA { GRAPH <http://g-union> { ex:o1 a ex:Office ; ex:city ex:c1 ; ex:code "001" . ex:x1 ex:office ex:o1 . } };

SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT DISTINCT ?direct ?link FROM <http://g-union> WHERE { ex:o1 a ex:Office . { ex:o1 ?link [] BIND(true AS ?direct) } UNION { [] ?link ex:o1 BIND(false AS ?direct) } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT DISTINCT ?direct ?link FROM <http://g-union> WHERE { ex:o1 a ex:Office . { ex:o1 ?link [] BIND(true AS ?direct) } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT DISTINCT ?direct ?link FROM <http://g-union> WHERE { ex:o1 a ex:Office . { [] ?link ex:o1 BIND(false AS ?direct) } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT DISTINCT ?link FROM <http://g-union> WHERE { ex:o1 ?link [] UNION { [] ?link ex:o1 } };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT DISTINCT ?direct ?link FROM <http://g-union> WHERE { { ex:o1 ?link [] BIND(true AS ?direct) } UNION { [] ?link ex:o1 BIND(false AS ?direct) } };

SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT DISTINCT ?direct ?link FROM <http://g-union> WHERE { ex:o1 a ex:Office . { ex:o1 ?link [] BIND(true AS ?direct) } UNION { [] ?link ex:o1 BIND(false AS ?direct) } FILTER(BOUND(?direct)) };
SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT DISTINCT ?direct ?link FROM <http://g-union> WHERE { ex:o1 a ex:Office . { ex:o1 ?link [] } UNION { [] ?link ex:o1 } BIND(true AS ?direct) };

SPARQL PREFIX ex: <http://example.org/> SELECT DISTINCT ?direct ?link FROM <http://g-union> WHERE { ex:o1 a ex:Office . { ex:o1 ?link [] BIND(true AS ?direct) } UNION { [] ?link ex:o1 BIND(false AS ?direct) } ORDER BY ?link };
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE office_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), location VARCHAR(255), active BOOLEAN);
INSERT INTO office_data (id, name, location, active) VALUES (1, 'Main Office', 'New York', TRUE);
INSERT INTO office_data (id, name, location, active) VALUES (2, 'Branch Office', 'Los Angeles', FALSE);

SELECT DISTINCT CASE WHEN active THEN 'true' ELSE 'false' END AS direct, location AS link FROM office_data WHERE active = TRUE UNION SELECT DISTINCT CASE WHEN active THEN 'true' ELSE 'false' END AS direct, location AS link FROM office_data WHERE active = FALSE;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM office_data WHERE location IS NOT NULL;
